problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
hypothesis: Monoclonal antibody blocking protein-protein interaction may resolve Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? by activating at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: In vitro cell culture assay with 31 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns. — Richard Feynman
